title: quercus infectoria var. latifolia
intro: Quercus infectoria var. latifolia, also known as the gall oak or Chinese gall, is a variety of Quercus infectoria, a deciduous tree native to parts of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the liver and improve digestion, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Quercus infectoria var. latifolia include its potential to treat conditions such as liver and gallbladder disorders, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its galls are also used in modern herbal preparations, such as capsules and tinctures.
